full_prompt: Design a topology-optimized Ti-6Al-4V AM bracket for a representative Renishaw/open-CAD platform 2025 aerospace bracket challenge (analogous in format to the GE Jet Engine Bracket Challenge but scaled for Renishaw RenAM 500Q four-laser LPBF machines and their published Ti-6Al-4V ELI data). Envelope: 180 x 120 x 80 mm. Interfaces: top planar flange with four 10 mm diameter bolt holes on 100 x 60 mm rectangular pattern (for M8 fasteners); bottom pin hole 20 mm diameter with axis perpendicular to the flange. Material: LPBF Renishaw Ti-6Al-4V ELI per Renishaw material data sheet, post-HIP'd (yield 860 MPa, UTS 930 MPa, E 113.8 GPa, density 4430 kg/m^3, elongation 10%). Load cases: LC1 vertical at pin hole 25 kN; LC2 horizontal 20 kN; LC3 off-axis 30 degrees from vertical 28 kN; LC4 torsional 3 kN*m about pin axis; LC5 sine vibration 10 g 5-500 Hz for qualification. Pass/fail per challenge rules (SF 1.5 on UTS, AM minimum feature, mass target): (R1) max von Mises any static case <= UTS / 1.5 = 620 MPa; (R2) max von Mises any static case <= yield / 1.15 = 748 MPa (looser, retained for traceability); (R3) mass target <= 400 g AND >= 50% lighter than bulk-billet baseline filling the 180x120x80 envelope; (R4) first linear buckling eigenvalue under LC3 >= 2.0; (R5) first natural frequency >= 150 Hz; (R6) AM manufacturability: min printable feature 0.3 mm (Renishaw RenAM 500Q typical), min overhang angle 45 deg, max enclosed void 8 mm; (R7) sine-vibration LC5 transmissibility-amplified peak stress <= 620 MPa; (R8) geometry must be single connected monolithic body, no supports internal to the part after HIP. Deliverables: STEP AP242 + 3MF + STL hi-res; per-case FEA report; modal + buckling analysis; mass vs baseline; submitted in Renishaw/open-CAD challenge-compatible archive with screenshot render per challenge submission rules.
short_prompt:
Design a topology-optimized Ti-6Al-4V AM bracket for a representative Renishaw/open-CAD 2025 aerospace bracket challenge scaled to Renishaw RenAM 500Q four-laser LPBF machines and their published Ti-6Al-4V ELI data.

Envelope: 180 x 120 x 80 mm. Interfaces: top planar flange with four 10 mm diameter bolt holes on 100 x 60 mm (for M8 fasteners); bottom 20 mm diameter pin hole with axis perpendicular to the flange.

Material: Renishaw LPBF Ti-6Al-4V ELI, post-HIP.

Design per challenge rules (SF 1.5 on UTS; AM minimum feature; mass target).

Load cases.
- LC1 vertical at pin hole: 25 kN.
- LC2 horizontal: 20 kN.
- LC3 off-axis 30 degrees from vertical: 28 kN.
- LC4 torsional 3 kN*m about the pin axis.
- LC5 sine vibration 10 g 5-500 Hz for qualification.

Gates: mass <= 400 g and >= 50% lighter than a bulk-billet baseline filling 180x120x80; first linear buckling eigenvalue under LC3 >= 2.0; first natural frequency >= 150 Hz; AM manufacturability (min feature 0.3 mm, min overhang 45 deg, max enclosed void 8 mm); single connected monolithic body with no internal supports after HIP.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: 50% mass reduction target drives topo-opt outer loop.
Build orientation + support minimization is AM-process optimization.

prompt.title: LPBF Ti-6Al-4V ELI aerospace bracket for Renishaw/open-CAD challenge
prompt.brief: Design a 180x120x80 mm LPBF Ti-6Al-4V ELI bracket passing 4 static + sine vibration with SF 1.5 on UTS, mass target 400 g, and >=50% reduction vs bulk billet.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: feature >=0.3 mm, overhang >=45 deg, void <=8 mm

analysis_types: topology optimization + linear static x4 + modal + buckling + sine

notes.numeric_source: Renishaw Ti-6Al-4V ELI AM material data sheet (yield 860 MPa, UTS 930 MPa post-HIP). Challenge load magnitudes scaled from jet-engine OEM challenge as representative values.
